texts.full_text: rental” for 3 months, during the busiest time of the year (Nov-April) you may want to throw caution to the wind and just take it. People rent homes for a few months without seeing them all the time, all over the world. If you have to rent a place during the high season without seeing it first, talk to us and let us help you find one that meets your needs – your wants may need to wait! Some folks do great looking ahead of time, some folks do better looking the moment they come – there is no exact recipe for success. Rentals vary in size, amenities, proximity to town, amount of neighbors, neighborhoods, plus more. For the best selection, check here. If you don’t see what you are looking for, contact us and let us know what you are looking for – sometimes rentals come and go before we can even list them! I can’t guarantee we will find you something, but we can try! Typically a 2BR/2BA home in the country, built to American Standards, costs about 1000/month. Sometimes there are several rentals, sometimes there are absolutely none. Usually the winter up north is the hardest time to secure a rental, and please do not come in June and expect a landlord to hold a rental for you until October, unless it is a long te

texts.full_text: host the db file on from a site like DropBox or Sharepoint365 where the path is an HTTP adYes, it is possible to host your Access database (`.accdb`) file online using services like Dropbox or SharePOint365 and access it through your Excel VBA application via DAO. Here are the general steps you would need to follow: 1. Upload the Database: First, upload your `.accdb` file to your chosen cloud service (Dropbox or SharePoint365). Make sure to note the HTTP URL of the shared folder containing the database file. 2. Modify Connection String: In your VBA code, you will need to modify the connection string to point to the new location of the database file. The connection string fOr DAO typically looks something like this: ```vba Dim db As DAO.Database Dim connString As String connString = "Driver={Microsoft Access Driver (.accdb)};" & _ "Dbq=" & "YOUR_HTTP_URL_HERE" & _ ";Extensions=.accdb" Set db = OpenDatabase(connString) ``` Replace `"YOUR_HTTP_URL_HERE"` with the actual HTTP URL of your shared folder containing the `.accdb` file. 3. Handle Authentication: If the cloud service requires authentication, you might need to include use

texts.full_text: a glimpse into my main spot for writing: TOP Left to right: Frazetta print; Unknown Artist, but it’s a mass of demonic frogs; Signed Clive Barker print of The Hell-PriestBottom: Left to Right:Cobra-La by Doktor Sewage; Ghoul gig poster (Are you Good? Or are you GHOUL Good??) I don’t feel quite right unless Wade is watching me, approving everything I do.Who is Wade?Uh…GOD… I mean, duh. Fucking told you it was signed. King Kong movie poster from France. I like this version better than the original US one by far.Right: A Peek Into My Reading Room It's been a while since my final post, caught up in life and work—or not <INSERT EXCUSE>. But yesterday inspiration struck me like lightning. Let’s make this tour of the space where I find solace amidst chaos. Here we go: Top left to Left: - A Frazetta print that echoes stories from a bygone era. A lesser-known composer's piece, but it brims with an ominous symphony—a mass chorus of demonic frogs. And there you have the Bob Davis masterpiece "The Hell-Priest," its dark beauty and seductive grace commanding each eye that descends on him. Bottom: - Doktor Sewage's Cobra-La, a whimsical yet comforting reminder of worlds beyond our own. And then the Ghoul gig poster—“Are you Good? Or are You GHOULGood??” It’s both chilling and inviting in its ambiguity. I don't look very right because James is seeing me over my knee, approving everything I do… Who's this e

texts.full_text: 2004, 01:44:37 AM » Hello everyone!I'm not only brand new to this forum, I'm brand new to beekeeping. I'm so brand new I don't even have a hive.I am a 30 yr old National Guard soldier living in Iraq and will be returning early next year to the states. I will be moving to Northern Utah (Cache Valley) and am interested in hearing from anyone in the state with good advice that is state specific.In real life (not Ar...life, not Army Ranger training), if you are a seasoned beekeeper living or near Cache Valley who has some experience specifically dealing w/ native species such as Western Honey Bees I would love to hear your thoughts and any recommendations.
